content.full_text:
March 4, 1991

Ms. Cheryl Hoff
FPPC -- Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Cheryl:

I've been advised that our deadline for the submission of an amended code is May 26, 1991. (I had mailed our "Biennial Report" on February 21, 1991.)

Based on our previous discussions, I would like to clarify the following points, in order to better understand what we will be asking our staff, directors and committee members to report:

1) Is there a basis for us to drop or rescind our Code because we are now covered by the State Small Business Development Program Code? You'll note that their code has a wider range of designated positions and the disclosure categories are similar. If we can't drop ours, do you see any problem with making ours a mirror image of theirs?

2) After sifting through the guidelines on consultants, it appears that our regular consultants (accountants, attorneys) do not have to make a disclosure because they possess no authority with respect to agency decisions, perform services for a single specific matter, ie annual audit, and their reports are subject to my review.

However, if it is determined that a particular consultant must disclose, must they file the full range of disclosures: Assuming, Annual and Leaving, or is there a special "one-time" disclosure for consultants?

3) One of our Directors is an attorney/small business consultant. Must he individually disclose the fees that he receives from his small business clients because of the SAFE-BIDCO category #1. Likewise, we have a Director that is a business broker and real estate salesman. Must he disclose commission income on each sale that he makes involving small business owners?

As always, your assistance is appreciated. Obviously your answers to this inquiry will have a major impact on the course of action we pursue.

Sincerely,

Paul O. Cormier
President

Enclosures
State Assistance Fund for Enterprise
Business and Industrial Development Corporation
